message:
type: SURVEY
files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Adding transformers parameter to Encoding.encode() (core.py) — a callable or list of callables (str -> str) applied sequentially to text before encoding, invoked at the start of encode()

type: SURVEY
files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Adding return_positions=True param to Encoding.encode() to track starting char position of each token

type: CLAIM
files: tiktoken/core.py
note: I'll own core.py and implement both features (return_positions in encode + your transformers param) since both touch the same encode() function body. Please send a SPEC for your transformers feature so I can implement it faithfully.

type: CLAIM
files: tiktoken/core.py
note: I'll own core.py since my transformers change is an isolated preprocessing step at the top of encode(); please DEFER and send a SPEC for return_positions so I implement both in one pass

type: DEFER
files: tiktoken/core.py
spec: Add a  keyword-only parameter to Encoding.encode(self, text, *, allowed_special=..., disallowed_special=..., transformers=None). Type: a single callable (str -> str) or a Sequence[Callable[[str], str]]. Behavior: at the very start of encode() (before the allowed_special/disallowed_special special-token handling and before calling self._core_bpe.encode), if transformers is not None, apply it to text: if it's a single callable (not a list/tuple), call text = transformers(text) once; if it's a list/tuple of callables, apply them sequentially in order, each receiving the previous one's output (text = fn(text) for fn in transformers). Default is None (no-op, fully backward compatible — existing behavior unchanged when transformers is omitted). No changes needed to encode_ordinary, encode_to_numpy, encode_batch, etc. — only plain encode(). Please also update the docstring with a brief note and example, e.g. enc.encode('HELLO', transformers=str.lower) -> tokens for 'hello'.
note: transformers param for Encoding.encode()

type: CLAIM
files: tiktoken/core.py
note: We both claimed simultaneously. Resolving: I'll take ownership since return_positions changes encode()'s return type/control-flow (conditional tuple return) which the transformers preprocessing step needs to compose with cleanly (transform -> encode -> position-tag). Please send your transformers SPEC (signature, param name, accepted types, order of application, error handling) and I'll implement both.

type: CLAIM
files: tiktoken/core.py
note: ACK received your transformers SPEC (keyword-only transformers=None, single callable or sequence, applied sequentially at start of encode() before special-token handling, backward compatible). I'll implement both features now and send DONE when finished.

type: DONE
files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Implemented both return_positions (mine) and transformers (your spec) in Encoding.encode(). Verified: encode(text, return_positions=True) returns (tokens, positions) matching decode_with_offsets; encode(text, transformers=str.lower) and list-of-callables both work; encode_batch/encode_ordinary unaffected. Existing tests pass. Wrote patch.txt.

type: DONE
files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Implemented transformers param in my own sandbox copy of core.py and wrote patch.txt (no shared filesystem visible between our sessions, so implemented independently per the submission protocol).
